title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. jucunda
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. jucunda, also known as the common columbine, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and relieve muscle cramps. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. jucunda include its ability to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
